trial_details.trajectory:
I'll help you fix the Kubernetes pod "test-client" in namespace "case13-triple". Let me start by diagnosing the problem.Good! The pod is running but the issue is that it's trying to connect to `webapp-svc` on port 80, which is failing. Let me check what services exist in this namespace and get more information.Aha! The issue is clear now. The `webapp` deployment's pod is in `ImagePullBackOff` status, which means it can't pull its image. This is why the `test-client` is failing to connect to `webapp-svc` - the webapp service has no running backend pods. Let me check the webapp deployment's image details.Perfect! I found the problem. The webapp deployment has a typo in the image name: `nginx:latets` should be `nginx:latest`. This is causing the ImagePullBackOff error, which means the webapp pod never starts, so the test-client cannot connect to it.

Now I'll fix the deployment by patching the image name:Perfect! The fix has been applied. Let me summarize what I found and fixed:

## Diagnosis
The `test-client` pod was running but failing to connect to `webapp-svc` because the `webapp` deployment's pod was stuck in `ImagePullBackOff` status. The root cause was a typo in the nginx image name: `nginx:latets` instead of `nginx:latest`. This prevented the webapp pod from starting, so there was no backend service to connect to.

## Fix Applied
I corrected the image name in the webapp deployment using:
```
kubectl set image deployment/webapp nginx=nginx:latest -n case13-triple
```

This command updated the deployment to use the correct image name. Kubernetes will now pull the correct `nginx:latest` image, the webapp pod will start, and the test-client will be able to successfully connect to the webapp service.

FIX COMPLETE
